steps.label: What we want to prove
steps.explanation: Given P and Q, prove P ∧ Q (P and Q). To prove a conjunction, we need to prove both sides.

steps.label: Apply ∧-Intro
steps.explanation: ∧-Introduction says: if you can prove P and you can prove Q, then you can prove P ∧ Q. We need both pieces — that's why conjunction means 'and'. Each premise sits above the inference bar.
